if (!require(shiny)) install.packages("shiny")
if (!require(shinyjs)) install.packages("shinyjs")

library(shiny)
library(shinyjs)

# ── HELPER: loose string similarity ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Returns 0–100% match based on word overlap (keyword matching)
score_answer <- function(user, correct) {
  if (nchar(trimws(user)) == 0) return(0)
  clean <- function(s) {
    s <- tolower(s)
    s <- gsub("[^a-z0-9 ]", " ", s)
    unique(unlist(strsplit(trimws(s), "\\s+")))
  }
  # Remove common stop words so content words drive the score
  stops <- c("a","an","the","is","are","was","were","in","of","to","and",
             "or","but","for","with","that","this","it","its","by","from",
             "as","at","on","be","can","may","not","more","also","both",
             "when","which","than","if","into","has","have","been","does",
             "do","used","using","only","most","each","all","any","some",
             "very","so","their","they","them","these","those","there","then",
             "will","would","should","could","per","e","g","i")
  uw <- setdiff(clean(user),    stops)
  cw <- setdiff(clean(correct), stops)
  if (length(cw) == 0) return(0)
  hits <- sum(uw %in% cw)
  round(100 * hits / length(cw))
}

# ── DATA ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
flashcards <- list(
  list(term="Randomised Controlled Trial (RCT)",
       definition="Participants randomly allocated to intervention or control; randomisation distributes known and unknown confounders. Gold standard for establishing causality.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Cluster Randomisation",
       definition="Groups (e.g. schools, GP practices) rather than individuals are randomised. Used to prevent contamination; cluster is the unit of analysis, requiring larger sample sizes.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Crossover Trial",
       definition="Participants receive both intervention and control sequentially, acting as their own control. Requires a washout period; only suitable when effects are rapid and reversible.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Intention to Treat (ITT)",
       definition="Participants analysed in their original randomised group regardless of actual treatment received. Preserves randomisation and reflects real-world effectiveness.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Per Protocol Analysis",
       definition="Analyses only participants who completed their allocated treatment. Provides the best estimate of efficacy but breaks randomisation, introducing potential confounding.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Efficacy vs Effectiveness",
       definition="Efficacy = benefit under ideal (controlled trial) conditions. Effectiveness = real-world benefit where adherence and delivery vary.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Cohort Study",
       definition="Exposed and unexposed groups are followed over time to measure incidence. Directly calculates relative risk; susceptible to loss to follow-up bias.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Case-Control Study",
       definition="Cases (with disease) and controls (without) are compared for prior exposures. Calculates odds ratio; efficient for rare diseases; susceptible to recall bias.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Cross-Sectional Study",
       definition="Exposure and outcome measured simultaneously — a 'snapshot'. Estimates prevalence but cannot establish temporality or causality.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Ecological Study",
       definition="Uses group-level (aggregate) data to examine associations. Cheap and uses routine data; key limitation is the ecological fallacy.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Ecological Fallacy",
       definition="Wrongly inferring individual-level associations from group-level data. The fundamental limitation of ecological studies.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Systematic Review",
       definition="Uses explicit, pre-specified methods to identify and synthesise all evidence on a question. More rigorous than a narrative review; quality depends on included studies.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Meta-Analysis",
       definition="Statistical pooling of results from multiple studies to produce a single summary estimate. Only appropriate when studies are sufficiently homogeneous.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Grey Literature",
       definition="Non-peer-reviewed material (reports, agency documents, websites) not identified by standard database searches. Must be sought in systematic reviews to reduce publication bias.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Heterogeneity (I²)",
       definition="Variability in results across studies beyond chance. I² <20% = minimal; 30–50% = moderate; >70% = severe. High I² makes pooling inappropriate.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Funnel Plot",
       definition="Plots effect size against sample size to detect publication bias. Asymmetry suggests smaller negative studies are missing.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Publication Bias",
       definition="Positive/significant results are more likely to be published. Inflates pooled effect estimates in meta-analyses; detected by funnel plot asymmetry.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Confounding",
       definition="A variable associated with both the exposure and outcome (but not on the causal pathway) that distorts the apparent association. Controlled by randomisation, restriction, matching, or multivariable analysis.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Selection Bias",
       definition="Systematic error in how participants are selected or retained, producing a non-representative sample. Examples: self-selection, loss to follow-up, healthy worker effect.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Information Bias",
       definition="Systematic error in measuring exposure or outcome. Includes recall bias (cases recall more than controls) and observer bias. Non-differential misclassification biases towards the null.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Bradford Hill Criteria",
       definition="Nine criteria to assess causality: strength, consistency, specificity, temporality, dose-response, plausibility, coherence, experiment, analogy. Temporality is the only essential criterion.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Relative Risk (Risk Ratio)",
       definition="Risk in exposed ÷ risk in unexposed. RR=1 means no association; RR>1 means increased risk. Directly calculated in cohort studies.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Odds Ratio",
       definition="Odds of exposure in cases ÷ odds of exposure in controls. Approximates RR when disease is rare. The measure of association from case-control studies.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Attributable Risk (Risk Difference)",
       definition="Incidence in exposed minus incidence in unexposed. Quantifies excess risk due to the exposure; useful for estimating public health impact.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Population Attributable Risk",
       definition="Reduction in incidence if the whole population were unexposed. Accounts for both strength of association and exposure prevalence; guides priority-setting.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Number Needed to Treat (NNT)",
       definition="1 ÷ absolute risk reduction. Patients needing treatment to prevent one additional adverse outcome. Lower = more effective treatment.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Absolute Risk Reduction (ARR)",
       definition="Risk in control group minus risk in intervention group. More informative than relative risk reduction for clinical decision-making.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Relative Risk Reduction (RRR)",
       definition="ARR ÷ control group risk. Always appears larger than ARR and can mislead if baseline risk is not stated.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Confidence Interval",
       definition="Range within which the true population value is estimated to lie (95% CI excludes null at p<0.05). Conveys both magnitude and precision of an estimate.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="P-Value",
       definition="Probability of results as extreme as observed if the null hypothesis were true. p<0.05 is statistically significant but does not indicate clinical importance.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Type I Error (α)",
       definition="Rejecting a true null hypothesis (false positive). Conventional threshold: α=0.05. Multiple comparisons inflate this risk.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Type II Error (β)",
       definition="Failing to reject a false null hypothesis (false negative). Power = 1–β; typically 80–90%.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Statistical Power",
       definition="Probability of detecting a true effect if one exists. Increases with sample size, effect size, and measurement precision.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Sample Size Calculation",
       definition="For prevalence studies: needs expected prevalence and desired CI width. For RCTs: needs effect size, SD, α, and power.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Kaplan-Meier Survival Analysis",
       definition="Non-parametric method estimating time-to-event probability, accounting for censoring. Groups compared using the log-rank test.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Cox Proportional Hazards Regression",
       definition="Semi-parametric survival model estimating hazard ratios for multiple covariates simultaneously. Assumes proportional hazards over time.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Logistic Regression",
       definition="Models binary outcomes as a function of multiple predictors. Produces adjusted odds ratios; used to control for confounders in observational studies.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Poisson Regression",
       definition="Models count data or incidence rates. Produces incidence rate ratios; used in ecological studies and time-series analysis of rare events.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Regression to the Mean",
       definition="Extreme values tend to return towards the mean on repeat measurement without intervention. A pitfall in uncontrolled before-and-after studies.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Sensitivity Analysis",
       definition="Tests robustness of findings by varying key assumptions or analytical choices. Stable conclusions across assumptions increase confidence in results.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Stratification",
       definition="Controlling for confounding by analysing the exposure-outcome association within subgroups defined by the confounder. Reduces power in each stratum.",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Matching",
       definition="Controls are selected to match cases on potential confounders. Matching must be accounted for in analysis (e.g. conditional logistic regression).",
       category="Research Methods"),
  list(term="Normal Distribution",
       definition="Symmetrical bell-shaped distribution. ~68% values within ±1 SD; ~95% within ±1.96 SDs. Assumed by parametric tests.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Skewed Distribution",
       definition="Asymmetrical distribution. Positive skew: mean > median (tail right, e.g. length of stay). Use median and IQR for skewed data.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Standard Deviation",
       definition="Square root of variance; measures spread of individual data around the mean.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Standard Error",
       definition="SD ÷ √n. Measures precision of the sample mean; used to calculate confidence intervals.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Interquartile Range (IQR)",
       definition="25th to 75th percentile — the middle 50% of data. Robust measure of spread for skewed or non-normal distributions.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Chi-Squared Test",
       definition="Tests association between two categorical variables. Requires expected frequencies ≥5; use Fisher's exact test otherwise.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Independent Samples t-Test",
       definition="Compares means of a continuous variable between two independent groups. Assumes normality and approximately equal variances.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Paired t-Test",
       definition="Compares means in the same subjects at two time points. Requires differences to be normally distributed.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Mann-Whitney U Test",
       definition="Non-parametric alternative to the independent t-test. Used when data are ordinal or non-normally distributed.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Pearson's Correlation (r)",
       definition="Measures strength and direction of a linear relationship (−1 to +1). Does not imply causality; r≈0 does not rule out non-linear associations.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Spearman's Rank Correlation",
       definition="Non-parametric correlation using ranked data. Used when data are ordinal or not normally distributed.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Forest Plot",
       definition="Displays individual study results and the pooled estimate in a meta-analysis. Box size ∝ study weight; diamond = pooled effect.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Outlier",
       definition="Observation far from the main data. Check for measurement/coding error first; if genuine, report sensitivity analysis including and excluding it.",
       category="Statistics"),
  list(term="Sensitivity",
       definition="True positives ÷ (true positives + false negatives). High sensitivity → good at ruling OUT disease (SnNout).",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Specificity",
       definition="True negatives ÷ (true negatives + false positives). High specificity → good at ruling IN disease (SpPin).",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Positive Predictive Value (PPV)",
       definition="True positives ÷ all positives. Falls with lower disease prevalence even if sensitivity and specificity are unchanged.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Negative Predictive Value (NPV)",
       definition="True negatives ÷ all negatives. Rises as prevalence falls.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Likelihood Ratio Positive (LR+)",
       definition="Sensitivity ÷ (1−specificity). LR+ >10 provides strong evidence for disease. Independent of prevalence.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Likelihood Ratio Negative (LR−)",
       definition="(1−sensitivity) ÷ specificity. LR− <0.1 provides strong evidence against disease. Independent of prevalence.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="ROC Curve",
       definition="Plots sensitivity vs. (1−specificity) across thresholds. AUC=1 is perfect; AUC=0.5 is no better than chance.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Wilson & Jungner Criteria",
       definition="Ten criteria for a population screening programme: important condition; recognisable latent stage; natural history understood; accepted treatment; facilities available; suitable test; agreed treatment policy; cost-effective; case-finding continuous; benefits outweigh harms.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Lead Time Bias",
       definition="Screen detection appears to extend survival only because the clock starts earlier, not because death is delayed. Overestimates screening benefit.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Length Time Bias",
       definition="Screening preferentially detects slow-growing, less aggressive tumours. Makes screen-detected cancers appear to have better prognosis.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Overdiagnosis",
       definition="Detection of cancers that would never have caused symptoms or death. Patients receive harmful treatment with no benefit. Major concern in PSA and breast screening.",
       category="Diagnostic Tests"),
  list(term="Incidence",
       definition="New cases arising in a defined population over a specified time. Measures risk of developing disease; used to evaluate preventive interventions.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Prevalence",
       definition="Proportion of a population with a condition at a given time. Prevalence ≈ incidence × duration. Used for planning chronic disease services.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Crude Death Rate",
       definition="Total deaths ÷ mid-year population × 1,000. Not adjusted for age structure; unsuitable for direct comparison between populations.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Direct Standardisation",
       definition="Age-specific rates of the study population applied to a standard population's age structure. Enables fair comparison across populations with different age distributions.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Indirect Standardisation / SMR",
       definition="Standard population's age-specific rates applied to study population's age structure. SMR = (observed ÷ expected) × 100; SMR >100 = excess mortality.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Years of Life Lost (YLL)",
       definition="Measures premature mortality by counting life-years lost relative to standard life expectancy. Weights deaths in younger people more heavily.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="DALY",
       definition="Years of Life Lost (YLL) + Years Lived with Disability (YLD), weighted by severity. One DALY = one year of healthy life lost.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="QALY",
       definition="Combines quantity and quality of life (0=death, 1=perfect health). NICE threshold ~£20,000–£30,000/QALY in England.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Life Expectancy",
       definition="Average years a person of a given age is expected to live based on current age-specific mortality rates. Requires age-specific mortality data and population estimates.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Healthy Life Expectancy",
       definition="Years expected to live in good health, combining mortality data with age-specific disability/health status. Requires multi-category disability weighting for full marks.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Compression of Morbidity",
       definition="Healthy life expectancy increases as a proportion of total life expectancy — people live longer AND healthier. Opposite = expansion of morbidity.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Person-Time",
       definition="Combines individuals and variable follow-up duration as the denominator for incidence rates (e.g. person-years). Allows valid rate calculation when follow-up times differ.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Attack Rate",
       definition="Cases ÷ exposed individuals × 100. Used in outbreak cohort studies to compare food-specific attack rates and identify the source.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Case Definition",
       definition="Standardised clinical, laboratory, time, place, and person criteria used to classify cases. Balance sensitivity (find all cases) vs. specificity (exclude non-cases).",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Epidemic Curve",
       definition="Histogram of case onset times. Narrow peak = point-source outbreak; propagated waves = person-to-person spread; plateau = continuous common source.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Basic Reproduction Number (R₀)",
       definition="Average secondary cases from one case in a fully susceptible population. R₀>1 → epidemic spreads. Determines herd immunity threshold: HIT = 1 − 1/R₀.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Herd Immunity Threshold",
       definition="Proportion of population that must be immune to interrupt transmission. HIT = 1 − 1/R₀. For measles (R₀ ≈ 12–18): ~94% immunity required.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Notifiable Disease",
       definition="Disease legally required to be reported to public health authorities upon diagnosis. Enables surveillance and timely public health response.",
       category="Epidemiology"),
  list(term="Meningococcal Disease",
       definition="Caused by Neisseria meningitidis; presents with fever, neck stiffness, photophobia, non-blanching rash. Manage with immediate benzylpenicillin; ciprofloxacin prophylaxis for close contacts.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="Measles",
       definition="Highly infectious paramyxovirus; incubation ~10 days; Koplik's spots then maculopapular rash. Herd immunity threshold ~94%; two MMR doses required.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="HPV",
       definition="Sexually transmitted; high-risk types 16 and 18 cause >70% of UK cervical cancers. UK immunisation programme (Gardasil) offered to all 12–13-year-olds.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="E. coli O157 (VTEC)",
       definition="Zoonotic infection; reservoir in cattle. Can cause haemolytic uraemic syndrome (HUS). Both HUS and infectious bloody diarrhoea are notifiable in England.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="Hepatitis C",
       definition="Blood-borne RNA virus; most commonly transmitted via injecting drug use. ~80% develop chronic infection; curative DAA therapy now available. No vaccine.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="Tuberculosis (TB)",
       definition="Mycobacterium tuberculosis; respiratory droplet transmission. Key risk factors: HIV, deprivation, migration from high-incidence countries. Control: notification, contact tracing, BCG, DOTS.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="Chlamydia",
       definition="Most common bacterial STI in the UK; mostly asymptomatic. Can cause PID, tubal infertility, and ectopic pregnancy. National Chlamydia Screening Programme in England.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="Vaccine Efficacy vs Effectiveness",
       definition="Efficacy = protection under ideal (trial) conditions. Effectiveness = real-world protection; may be lower due to cold chain, adherence, and population differences.",
       category="Infectious Diseases"),
  list(term="Cancer Registry",
       definition="Database of all new cancer cases in a defined population. Used for surveillance, service planning, evaluating screening, and research. Raises data protection concerns.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Disease Register",
       definition="Database of individuals with a long-term condition. Key features: defined population, clear case definition, high registration rate. Used for audit, recall, and service planning.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Passive Surveillance",
       definition="Clinicians/labs report notifiable cases as they occur. Cheap but under-reports due to incomplete case ascertainment and variable reporting behaviour.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Active Surveillance",
       definition="Public health authorities proactively contact providers to seek case information. More complete and timely than passive surveillance but more resource-intensive.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Sentinel Surveillance",
       definition="Selected representative sites (sentinels) monitor trends over time. Efficient but not population-representative. Example: RCGP influenza surveillance network.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Total Fertility Rate (TFR)",
       definition="Average number of children a woman would have in her lifetime at current age-specific fertility rates. TFR ~2.1 = replacement level in high-income countries.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Information Governance",
       definition="Framework of law and policy ensuring personal information is managed securely and confidentially. Key elements: GDPR, Data Protection Act, Caldicott Guardian, electronic security.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Routine Data Sources",
       definition="Administrative data from routine service delivery (e.g. HES, death certificates, cancer registrations, QOF). Advantages: large, continuous, cheap. Disadvantages: limited variables, coding errors.",
       category="Health Information"),
  list(term="Opportunity Cost",
       definition="The value of the next best alternative foregone when a resource allocation decision is made. The fundamental concept underlying all economic evaluation in healthcare.",
       category="Health Economics"),
  list(term="Discounting",
       definition="Future costs and benefits adjusted to present value (UK discount rate 3.5%/year). Critical in screening: costs now, benefits decades later.",
       category="Health Economics"),
  list(term="Cost-Effectiveness Analysis",
       definition="Compares costs against health outcomes (e.g. cost per life-year gained). Does not assign monetary values to health; enables comparison of similar interventions.",
       category="Health Economics"),
  list(term="Cost-Utility Analysis",
       definition="Measures outcomes in QALYs; results as cost per QALY. NICE threshold ~£20,000–£30,000/QALY in England.",
       category="Health Economics"),
  list(term="Cost-Benefit Analysis",
       definition="All costs and outcomes valued in monetary terms. Enables comparison with non-health investments but ethically difficult to monetise human life.",
       category="Health Economics"),
  list(term="Marginal Analysis / PBMA",
       definition="Examines the additional cost and benefit of expanding/reducing a programme by one unit. Used in PBMA to guide resource reallocation decisions.",
       category="Health Economics"),
  list(term="Return on Investment (ROI)",
       definition="(Benefit − Cost) ÷ Cost. Used in public health to demonstrate value of preventive programmes. Social Return on Investment (SROI) includes wider societal benefits.",
       category="Health Economics"),
  list(term="Sick Role (Parsons)",
       definition="Rights: exempt from blame; exempt from normal responsibilities. Obligations: want to recover; seek medical help. Criticised for not accounting for chronic illness.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Social Iatrogenesis (Illich)",
       definition="Medicalisation undermines individuals' capacity to cope with illness independently. Part of Illich's broader critique of the 'industrialisation' of medicine.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Social Model of Disability",
       definition="Disability caused by societal barriers (physical, attitudinal, organisational), not the impairment itself. Contrasts with the medical model (problem within individual).",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Medical Model of Disability",
       definition="Disability seen as individual deficit requiring compensation or treatment. Leads to segregated services; criticised for fostering dependence and reducing autonomy.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Health Locus of Control",
       definition="Internal: health determined by own behaviour. External: determined by chance or powerful others. Internal locus associated with greater uptake of preventive behaviours.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Inverse Care Law (Tudor Hart)",
       definition="Those in greatest need have least access to good-quality care. Key driver of health inequalities in both primary and secondary care.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Dahlgren & Whitehead Rainbow Model",
       definition="Concentric layers of health determinants: individual biology → lifestyle → social networks → living/working conditions → macroeconomic and environmental context.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Health Inequalities",
       definition="Systematic, avoidable, unfair differences in health between social groups. Driven by social determinants. Marmot Review (2010) identified life course action as key.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Social Determinants of Health",
       definition="Conditions in which people are born, grow, live, work, and age. Include income, education, employment, and housing. Primary drivers of health inequalities.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Globalisation and Health",
       definition="Political: constrains national health policy (WTO/EU rules). Economic: global health workforce migration, multinational pharma pricing. Social: raised expectations, dietary westernisation.",
       category="Medical Sociology"),
  list(term="Ottawa Charter",
       definition="WHO 1986: five action areas — build healthy public policy; create supportive environments; strengthen community action; develop personal skills; reorient health services.",
       category="Health Promotion"),
  list(term="Stages of Change (Transtheoretical Model)",
       definition="Five stages: precontemplation → contemplation → preparation → action → maintenance. Interventions should be tailored to current stage; relapse is normal.",
       category="Health Promotion"),
  list(term="Health Belief Model",
       definition="Behaviour determined by perceived susceptibility, severity, benefits, barriers, cues to action, and self-efficacy. Widely used in vaccination and screening uptake interventions.",
       category="Health Promotion"),
  list(term="Needs Assessment",
       definition="Three approaches: epidemiological (incidence/prevalence + evidence); comparative (benchmarking); corporate (stakeholder views). Informs commissioning decisions.",
       category="Health Promotion"),
  list(term="Health Impact Assessment (HIA)",
       definition="Prospective appraisal of health impacts of a policy or project. Stages: screening → scoping → appraisal → reporting → monitoring. Considers vulnerable groups.",
       category="Health Promotion"),
  list(term="Social Marketing",
       definition="Applies commercial marketing principles to voluntary behaviour change. Key principles: customer orientation, segmentation, exchange theory, insight, competition.",
       category="Health Promotion"),
  list(term="Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs",
       definition="Five-level hierarchy: physiological → safety → social/belonging → esteem → self-actualisation. Lower-order needs must be broadly met before higher-order needs motivate.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Herzberg's Two-Factor Theory",
       definition="Motivators (intrinsic: achievement, recognition, responsibility) drive satisfaction. Hygiene factors (extrinsic: pay, conditions) prevent dissatisfaction but do not motivate.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="McGregor Theory X and Theory Y",
       definition="Theory X: workers are lazy, require control. Theory Y: workers are self-motivated, thrive with autonomy. Management style shapes culture and performance.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Tuckman's Group Development",
       definition="Five stages: forming → storming → norming → performing → adjourning. Leaders should adapt style to the team's developmental stage.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="SMART Targets",
       definition="Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound. Disadvantages: may incentivise gaming; focus on measurable at expense of important; targets may conflict.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Strategy vs Policy",
       definition="Strategy = high-level plan setting direction and goals. Policy = agreed set of measures (e.g. legislation, guidance) contributing to the strategy.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Donabedian Model",
       definition="Structure (resources) → Process (clinical activities) → Outcome (health impact). Framework for healthcare quality evaluation. Structure is necessary but not sufficient.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Clinical Governance",
       definition="Framework for NHS accountability and continuous quality improvement. Components: clinical audit, evidence-based practice, risk management, patient involvement, training.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Logic Model",
       definition="Depicts inputs → activities → outputs → short/medium/long-term outcomes. Makes the theory of change explicit and identifies appropriate evaluation measures.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="NHS Breast Screening",
       definition="3-yearly mammography for women aged 50–70. ~20% mortality reduction in invited women. Harms: overdiagnosis and false positive biopsies.",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="NHS Cervical Screening",
       definition="Primary HPV testing with cytology triage. Ages 25–49: 3-yearly; 50–64: 5-yearly. Detects CIN (pre-invasive lesions). HPV vaccination will reduce future screening need.",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="NHS Bowel Cancer Screening",
       definition="FIT offered to adults aged 50–74; colonoscopy if positive. Detects colorectal cancer early and removes adenomatous polyps.",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="NHS AAA Screening",
       definition="Single ultrasound scan for men aged 65. Detects asymptomatic aneurysms; rupture carries ~80% mortality. Women excluded (6× lower risk than men).",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="Marmot Review (2010)",
       definition="Health inequalities are driven by social inequalities and are avoidable. Six policy objectives across the life course, with emphasis on early years and employment.",
       category="Social Policy"),
  list(term="Acheson Report (1998)",
       definition="Independent inquiry documenting widening UK health inequalities. Prioritised policies for families with children, income inequality, and health impact evaluation of all policies.",
       category="Social Policy"),
  list(term="Black Report (1980)",

# ── SERVER ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
  rv <- reactiveValues(
    tab           = "study",
    # study
    deck          = seq_len(N),
    cur           = 1L,
    revealed      = FALSE,
    statuses      = rep("unseen", N),
    # quiz
    quiz_deck     = seq_len(N),
    quiz_cur      = 1L,
    quiz_revealed = FALSE,   # TRUE = answer submitted, showing comparison
    quiz_done     = FALSE,
    quiz_score    = 0L       # keyword overlap score for current card
  )
  
  # ── Flags for conditionalPanel
  output$active_tab <- reactive({ rv$tab })
  output$quiz_done  <- reactive({ if (rv$quiz_done) "yes" else "no" })
  outputOptions(output, "active_tab", suspendWhenHidden=FALSE)
  outputOptions(output, "quiz_done",  suspendWhenHidden=FALSE)
  
  output$badge_txt <- renderText({ paste(N, "Cards") })
  
  # ── Tab switching
  switch_tab <- function(t) {
    rv$tab <- t
    i <- switch(t, study=0L, quiz=1L, results=2L)
    runjs(sprintf(
      "document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(b=>b.classList.remove('active'));
       document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn')[%d].classList.add('active');", i))
  }
  observeEvent(input$tab_study,   switch_tab("study"))
  observeEvent(input$tab_quiz,    switch_tab("quiz"))
  observeEvent(input$tab_results, switch_tab("results"))
  
  # ── Build deck
  build_deck <- function() {
    cat <- input$cat_filter; ord <- input$order_mode
    idx <- seq_len(N)
    if (!is.null(cat) && cat != "ALL")
      idx <- idx[sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$category == cat)]
    if (ord == "rand") idx <- sample(idx)
    else if (ord == "inc") {
      bad <- idx[rv$statuses[idx]=="incorrect"]
      idx <- c(bad, idx[rv$statuses[idx]!="incorrect"])
    }
    idx
  }
  
  observeEvent(list(input$cat_filter, input$order_mode), {
    rv$deck <- build_deck(); rv$cur <- 1L; rv$revealed <- FALSE
  }, ignoreInit=TRUE)
  
  observeEvent(input$restart_btn, {
    rv$statuses      <- rep("unseen", N)
    rv$deck          <- build_deck(); rv$cur <- 1L; rv$revealed <- FALSE
    rv$quiz_deck     <- build_deck(); rv$quiz_cur <- 1L
    rv$quiz_revealed <- FALSE; rv$quiz_done <- FALSE; rv$quiz_score <- 0L
    # clear the textarea
    runjs("var ta=document.getElementById('quiz_answer'); if(ta) ta.value='';")
  })
  
  cur_card  <- reactive({ flashcards[[ rv$deck[rv$cur] ]] })
  quiz_card <- reactive({ flashcards[[ rv$quiz_deck[rv$quiz_cur] ]] })
  
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # STUDY
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  observeEvent(input$reveal_btn, { rv$revealed <- TRUE })
  observeEvent(input$card_click, { rv$revealed <- TRUE })
  
  do_advance <- function(status) {
    rv$statuses[ rv$deck[rv$cur] ] <- status
    if (rv$cur < length(rv$deck)) rv$cur <- rv$cur + 1L
    rv$revealed <- FALSE
  }
  observeEvent(input$btn_correct,   do_advance("correct"))
  observeEvent(input$btn_incorrect, do_advance("incorrect"))
  observeEvent(input$btn_skip, {
    if (rv$statuses[rv$deck[rv$cur]]=="unseen") rv$statuses[rv$deck[rv$cur]] <- "skipped"
    do_advance("skipped")
  })
  observeEvent(input$btn_prev, {
    if (rv$cur > 1L) rv$cur <- rv$cur - 1L
    rv$revealed <- FALSE
  })
  
  output$study_counter <- renderText({ paste(rv$cur,"/",length(rv$deck)) })
  output$study_cat     <- renderText({ cur_card()$category })
  
  output$study_progress <- renderUI({
    n <- length(rv$deck); cor <- sum(rv$statuses[rv$deck]=="correct")
    pct <- if(n>0) round(100*cor/n) else 0
    tagList(
      div(class="progress-outer", div(class="progress-inner",style=paste0("width:",pct,"%;"))),
      div(class="progress-txt", paste0(cor," / ",n," correct (",pct,"%)"))
    )
  })
  
  output$study_card_body <- renderUI({
    card <- cur_card()
    if (!rv$revealed) {
      tagList(div(class="card-term",card$term),
              div(class="card-hint","Click the card or press Reveal to see the definition"))
    } else {
      tagList(div(class="card-term",card$term),
              tags$hr(class="card-divider"),
              div(class="card-def",card$definition))
    }
  })
  
  output$study_buttons <- renderUI({
    if (!rv$revealed) {
      tagList(
        div(class="btn-row",
            tags$button("Reveal Definition", class="btn-reveal",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('reveal_btn',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")),
        div(class="btn-row",style="margin-top:10px;",
            tags$button("← Previous",class="btn-nav",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_prev',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
            tags$button("Skip →",class="btn-nav",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_skip',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"))
      )
    } else {
      tagList(
        div(class="btn-row",
            tags$button("✓ Got it",class="btn-correct",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_correct',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
            tags$button("✗ Review again",class="btn-incorrect",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_incorrect',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
        ),
        div(class="btn-row",style="margin-top:10px;",
            tags$button("← Previous",class="btn-nav",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_prev',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
            tags$button("Skip →",class="btn-nav",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_skip',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"))
      )
    }
  })
  
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # QUIZ
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  
  # Submit answer: score it then reveal
  observeEvent(input$quiz_submit_btn, {
    user  <- if (!is.null(input$quiz_answer)) input$quiz_answer else ""
    score <- score_answer(user, quiz_card()$definition)
    rv$quiz_score    <- score
    rv$quiz_revealed <- TRUE
  })
  
  # Also allow Enter key (Shift+Enter = newline, plain Enter = submit)
  # handled via JS injected below
  
  quiz_advance <- function(status) {
    rv$statuses[ rv$quiz_deck[rv$quiz_cur] ] <- status
    if (rv$quiz_cur < length(rv$quiz_deck)) {
      rv$quiz_cur      <- rv$quiz_cur + 1L
      rv$quiz_revealed <- FALSE
      rv$quiz_score    <- 0L
      # clear textarea
      runjs("var ta=document.getElementById('quiz_answer'); if(ta){ ta.value=''; ta.focus(); }")
    } else {
      rv$quiz_done <- TRUE
    }
  }
  observeEvent(input$quiz_mark_correct,   quiz_advance("correct"))
  observeEvent(input$quiz_mark_incorrect, quiz_advance("incorrect"))
  
  output$quiz_counter <- renderText({ paste(rv$quiz_cur,"/",length(rv$quiz_deck)) })
  output$quiz_cat     <- renderText({ quiz_card()$category })
  
  output$quiz_progress <- renderUI({
    n <- length(rv$quiz_deck); done <- rv$quiz_cur - 1L
    pct <- if(n>0) round(100*done/n) else 0
    tagList(
      div(class="progress-outer", div(class="progress-inner",style=paste0("width:",pct,"%;"))),
      div(class="progress-txt", paste0(done," / ",n," answered (",pct,"%)"))
    )
  })
  
  output$quiz_panel <- renderUI({
    card  <- quiz_card()
    score <- rv$quiz_score
    
    # Colour and label for the score meter
    bar_col <- if(score >= 60) "#27ae60" else if(score >= 30) "#f39c12" else "#e74c3c"
    verdict <- if(score >= 60) paste0("Good match — ",score,"% keyword overlap")
    else if(score >= 30) paste0("Partial match — ",score,"% keyword overlap")
    else if(score > 0)   paste0("Low match — ",score,"% keyword overlap")
    else ""
    
    tagList(
      div(class="quiz-shell",
          div(class="quiz-counter", textOutput("quiz_counter", inline=TRUE)),
          div(class="quiz-badge",   textOutput("quiz_cat",     inline=TRUE)),
          
          # Term
          div(class="quiz-term", card$term),
          div(class="quiz-cat",  card$category),
          
          # Answer textarea (always rendered; hidden after submit via CSS pointer-events)
          tags$textarea(
            id          = "quiz_answer",
            class       = "quiz-input",
            placeholder = "Type your definition here, then press Submit Answer…",
            style       = if (rv$quiz_revealed) "opacity:0.55;pointer-events:none;" else ""
          ),
          
          # Score meter (only after submit)
          if (rv$quiz_revealed) {
            div(class="score-meter-wrap",
                div(class="score-meter-outer",
                    div(class="score-meter-inner",
                        style=paste0("width:",score,"%;background:",bar_col,";"))),
                div(class="score-label", style=paste0("color:",bar_col,";"), verdict)
            )
          },
          
          # Model answer (only after submit)
          if (rv$quiz_revealed) {
            div(class="answer-box",
                div(class="answer-box-title", "Model Answer"),
                card$definition
            )
          }
      ),
      
      # Buttons
      if (!rv$quiz_revealed) {
        div(class="btn-row",
            tags$button("Submit Answer", class="btn-submit",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('quiz_submit_btn',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
        )
      } else {
        div(class="btn-row",
            tags$button("✓ Mark Correct", class="btn-correct",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('quiz_mark_correct',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
            tags$button("✗ Mark Incorrect", class="btn-incorrect",
                        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('quiz_mark_incorrect',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
        )
      }
    )
  })
  
  output$completion_ui <- renderUI({
    n   <- length(rv$quiz_deck)
    cor <- sum(rv$statuses[rv$quiz_deck]=="correct")
    pct <- if(n>0) round(100*cor/n) else 0
    grade <- if(pct>=80)"🎉 Excellent!" else if(pct>=60)"👍 Good effort" else "📖 Keep reviewing"
    div(class="completion-panel",
        div(class="completion-title", grade),
        div(class="completion-pct",   paste0(pct,"%")),
        div(style="font-size:18px;color:#555;", paste0(cor," correct out of ",n," cards")),
        br(),
        tags$button("Retake Quiz", class="btn-restart",
                    style="font-size:16px;padding:12px 32px;",
                    onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('restart_btn',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
    )
  })
  
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # RESULTS
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  results_df <- reactive({
    cat <- input$cat_filter; idx <- seq_len(N)
    if (!is.null(cat) && cat != "ALL")
      idx <- idx[sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$category == cat)]
    data.frame(
      Term     = sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$term),
      Category = sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$category),
      Status   = rv$statuses[idx],
      stringsAsFactors=FALSE
    )
  })
  
  output$r_total   <- renderText({ nrow(results_df()) })
  output$r_correct <- renderText({ sum(results_df()$Status=="correct") })
  output$r_wrong   <- renderText({ sum(results_df()$Status=="incorrect") })
  output$r_skipped <- renderText({ sum(results_df()$Status=="skipped") })
  output$r_pct <- renderText({
    df <- results_df(); seen <- df$Status %in% c("correct","incorrect")
    if (!any(seen)) return("–")
    paste0(round(100*sum(df$Status=="correct")/sum(seen)),"%")
  })
  
  output$results_tbl <- renderUI({
    df <- results_df()
    
    status_html <- function(s) {
      switch(s,
             "correct"   = '<span style="color:#27ae60;font-weight:700;">✓ Correct</span>',
             "incorrect" = '<span style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:700;">✗ Review</span>',
             "skipped"   = '<span style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:700;">→ Skipped</span>',
             '<span style="color:#aaa;font-weight:600;">Unseen</span>'
      )
    }
    
    ord <- c("incorrect","unseen","skipped","correct")
    df  <- df[order(match(df$Status, ord)), ]
    
    rows <- paste0(
      apply(df, 1, function(r) {
        paste0(
          "<tr>",
          "<td style='padding:9px 12px;font-size:13px;border-bottom:1px solid #f0f0f0;'>",
          htmltools::htmlEscape(r["Term"]), "</td>",
          "<td style='padding:9px 12px;font-size:13px;border-bottom:1px solid #f0f0f0;'>",
          htmltools::htmlEscape(r["Category"]), "</td>",
          "<td style='padding:9px 12px;font-size:13px;border-bottom:1px solid #f0f0f0;'>",
          status_html(r["Status"]), "</td>",
          "</tr>"
        )
      }),
      collapse=""
    )
    
    HTML(paste0(
      '<table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;">',
      '<thead><tr>',
      '<th style="background:#f0f4fa;color:#1a3c5e;padding:10px 12px;font-size:13px;',
      'border-bottom:2px solid #dde6f0;text-align:left;">Term</th>',
      '<th style="background:#f0f4fa;color:#1a3c5e;padding:10px 12px;font-size:13px;',
      'border-bottom:2px solid #dde6f0;text-align:left;">Category</th>',
      '<th style="background:#f0f4fa;color:#1a3c5e;padding:10px 12px;font-size:13px;',
      'border-bottom:2px solid #dde6f0;text-align:left;">Status</th>',
      '</tr></thead>',
      '<tbody>', rows, '</tbody>',
      '</table>'
    ))
  })
  
}  # closes server function

shinyApp(
  options = list(height = "900px"),
  ui=ui, server=server)

---

    theme: flatly

```{r libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include = FALSE}
librarian::shelf(shiny, shinyjs)
```

```{r data}
# ── HELPER: loose string similarity ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Returns 0–100% match based on word overlap (keyword matching)
score_answer <- function(user, correct) {
  if (nchar(trimws(user)) == 0) return(0)
  clean <- function(s) {
    s <- tolower(s)
    s <- gsub("[^a-z0-9 ]", " ", s)
    unique(unlist(strsplit(trimws(s), "\\s+")))
  }
  # Remove common stop words so content words drive the score
  stops <- c("a","an","the","is","are","was","were","in","of","to","and",
             "or","but","for","with","that","this","it","its","by","from",
             "as","at","on","be","can","may","not","more","also","both",
             "when","which","than","if","into","has","have","been","does",
             "do","used","using","only","most","each","all","any","some",
             "very","so","their","they","them","these","those","there","then",
             "will","would","should","could","per","e","g","i")
  uw <- setdiff(clean(user),    stops)
  cw <- setdiff(clean(correct), stops)
  if (length(cw) == 0) return(0)
  hits <- sum(uw %in% cw)
  round(100 * hits / length(cw))
}

       definition="Five stages: forming → storming → norming → performing → adjourning. Leaders should adapt style to the team's developmental stage.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="SMART Targets",
       definition="Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound. Disadvantages: may incentivise gaming; focus on measurable at expense of important; targets may conflict.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Strategy vs Policy",
       definition="Strategy = high-level plan setting direction and goals. Policy = agreed set of measures (e.g. legislation, guidance) contributing to the strategy.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Donabedian Model",
       definition="Structure (resources) → Process (clinical activities) → Outcome (health impact). Framework for healthcare quality evaluation. Structure is necessary but not sufficient.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Clinical Governance",
       definition="Framework for NHS accountability and continuous quality improvement. Components: clinical audit, evidence-based practice, risk management, patient involvement, training.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="Logic Model",
       definition="Depicts inputs → activities → outputs → short/medium/long-term outcomes. Makes the theory of change explicit and identifies appropriate evaluation measures.",
       category="Management"),
  list(term="NHS Breast Screening",
       definition="3-yearly mammography for women aged 50–70. ~20% mortality reduction in invited women. Harms: overdiagnosis and false positive biopsies.",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="NHS Cervical Screening",
       definition="Primary HPV testing with cytology triage. Ages 25–49: 3-yearly; 50–64: 5-yearly. Detects CIN (pre-invasive lesions). HPV vaccination will reduce future screening need.",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="NHS Bowel Cancer Screening",
       definition="FIT offered to adults aged 50–74; colonoscopy if positive. Detects colorectal cancer early and removes adenomatous polyps.",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="NHS AAA Screening",
       definition="Single ultrasound scan for men aged 65. Detects asymptomatic aneurysms; rupture carries ~80% mortality. Women excluded (6× lower risk than men).",
       category="Screening"),
  list(term="Marmot Review (2010)",
       definition="Health inequalities are driven by social inequalities and are avoidable. Six policy objectives across the life course, with emphasis on early years and employment.",
       category="Social Policy"),
  list(term="Acheson Report (1998)",
       definition="Independent inquiry documenting widening UK health inequalities. Prioritised policies for families with children, income inequality, and health impact evaluation of all policies.",
       category="Social Policy"),
  list(term="Black Report (1980)",
       definition="Documented social class gradient in UK health. Four explanations: artefact; social selection; cultural/behavioural; materialist/structural (most important).",
       category="Social Policy"),
  list(term="Wanless Report (2002)",
       definition="Modelled three NHS funding scenarios; 'fully engaged' (high public health investment) was most cost-effective. Highlighted importance of evidence-based public health spending.",
       category="Social Policy")
)

categories <- sort(unique(sapply(flashcards, function(x) x$category)))

# ── SERVER ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
server <- function(input, output, session) {

  rv <- reactiveValues(
    tab           = "study",
    # study
    deck          = seq_len(N),
    cur           = 1L,
    revealed      = FALSE,
    statuses      = rep("unseen", N),
    # quiz
    quiz_deck     = seq_len(N),
    quiz_cur      = 1L,
    quiz_revealed = FALSE,   # TRUE = answer submitted, showing comparison
    quiz_done     = FALSE,
    quiz_score    = 0L       # keyword overlap score for current card
  )

  # ── Flags for conditionalPanel
  output$active_tab <- reactive({ rv$tab })
  output$quiz_done  <- reactive({ if (rv$quiz_done) "yes" else "no" })
  outputOptions(output, "active_tab", suspendWhenHidden=FALSE)
  outputOptions(output, "quiz_done",  suspendWhenHidden=FALSE)

  output$badge_txt <- renderText({ paste(N, "Cards") })

  # ── Tab switching
  switch_tab <- function(t) {
    rv$tab <- t
    i <- switch(t, study=0L, quiz=1L, results=2L)
    runjs(sprintf(
      "document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(b=>b.classList.remove('active'));
       document.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn')[%d].classList.add('active');", i))
  }
  observeEvent(input$tab_study,   switch_tab("study"))
  observeEvent(input$tab_quiz,    switch_tab("quiz"))
  observeEvent(input$tab_results, switch_tab("results"))

  # ── Build deck
  build_deck <- function() {
    cat <- input$cat_filter; ord <- input$order_mode
    idx <- seq_len(N)
    if (!is.null(cat) && cat != "ALL")
      idx <- idx[sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$category == cat)]
    if (ord == "rand") idx <- sample(idx)
    else if (ord == "inc") {
      bad <- idx[rv$statuses[idx]=="incorrect"]
      idx <- c(bad, idx[rv$statuses[idx]!="incorrect"])
    }
    idx
  }

  observeEvent(list(input$cat_filter, input$order_mode), {
    rv$deck <- build_deck(); rv$cur <- 1L; rv$revealed <- FALSE
  }, ignoreInit=TRUE)

  observeEvent(input$restart_btn, {
    rv$statuses      <- rep("unseen", N)
    rv$deck          <- build_deck(); rv$cur <- 1L; rv$revealed <- FALSE
    rv$quiz_deck     <- build_deck(); rv$quiz_cur <- 1L
    rv$quiz_revealed <- FALSE; rv$quiz_done <- FALSE; rv$quiz_score <- 0L
    # clear the textarea
    runjs("var ta=document.getElementById('quiz_answer'); if(ta) ta.value='';")
  })

  cur_card  <- reactive({ flashcards[[ rv$deck[rv$cur] ]] })
  quiz_card <- reactive({ flashcards[[ rv$quiz_deck[rv$quiz_cur] ]] })

  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # STUDY
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  observeEvent(input$reveal_btn, { rv$revealed <- TRUE })
  observeEvent(input$card_click, { rv$revealed <- TRUE })

  do_advance <- function(status) {
    rv$statuses[ rv$deck[rv$cur] ] <- status
    if (rv$cur < length(rv$deck)) rv$cur <- rv$cur + 1L
    rv$revealed <- FALSE
  }
  observeEvent(input$btn_correct,   do_advance("correct"))
  observeEvent(input$btn_incorrect, do_advance("incorrect"))
  observeEvent(input$btn_skip, {
    if (rv$statuses[rv$deck[rv$cur]]=="unseen") rv$statuses[rv$deck[rv$cur]] <- "skipped"
    do_advance("skipped")
  })
  observeEvent(input$btn_prev, {
    if (rv$cur > 1L) rv$cur <- rv$cur - 1L
    rv$revealed <- FALSE
  })

  output$study_counter <- renderText({ paste(rv$cur,"/",length(rv$deck)) })
  output$study_cat     <- renderText({ cur_card()$category })

  output$study_progress <- renderUI({
    n <- length(rv$deck); cor <- sum(rv$statuses[rv$deck]=="correct")
    pct <- if(n>0) round(100*cor/n) else 0
    tagList(
      div(class="progress-outer", div(class="progress-inner",style=paste0("width:",pct,"%;"))),
      div(class="progress-txt", paste0(cor," / ",n," correct (",pct,"%)"))
    )
  })

  output$study_card_body <- renderUI({
    card <- cur_card()
    if (!rv$revealed) {
      tagList(div(class="card-term",card$term),
              div(class="card-hint","Click the card or press Reveal to see the definition"))
    } else {
      tagList(div(class="card-term",card$term),
              tags$hr(class="card-divider"),
              div(class="card-def",card$definition))
    }
  })

  output$study_buttons <- renderUI({
    if (!rv$revealed) {
      tagList(
        div(class="btn-row",
          tags$button("Reveal Definition", class="btn-reveal",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('reveal_btn',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")),
        div(class="btn-row",style="margin-top:10px;",
          tags$button("← Previous",class="btn-nav",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_prev',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
          tags$button("Skip →",class="btn-nav",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_skip',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"))
      )
    } else {
      tagList(
        div(class="btn-row",
          tags$button("✓ Got it",class="btn-correct",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_correct',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
          tags$button("✗ Review again",class="btn-incorrect",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_incorrect',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
        ),
        div(class="btn-row",style="margin-top:10px;",
          tags$button("← Previous",class="btn-nav",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_prev',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
          tags$button("Skip →",class="btn-nav",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('btn_skip',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"))
      )
    }
  })

  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # QUIZ
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  # Submit answer: score it then reveal
  observeEvent(input$quiz_submit_btn, {
    user  <- if (!is.null(input$quiz_answer)) input$quiz_answer else ""
    score <- score_answer(user, quiz_card()$definition)
    rv$quiz_score    <- score
    rv$quiz_revealed <- TRUE
  })

  # Also allow Enter key (Shift+Enter = newline, plain Enter = submit)
  # handled via JS injected below

  quiz_advance <- function(status) {
    rv$statuses[ rv$quiz_deck[rv$quiz_cur] ] <- status
    if (rv$quiz_cur < length(rv$quiz_deck)) {
      rv$quiz_cur      <- rv$quiz_cur + 1L
      rv$quiz_revealed <- FALSE
      rv$quiz_score    <- 0L
      # clear textarea
      runjs("var ta=document.getElementById('quiz_answer'); if(ta){ ta.value=''; ta.focus(); }")
    } else {
      rv$quiz_done <- TRUE
    }
  }
  observeEvent(input$quiz_mark_correct,   quiz_advance("correct"))
  observeEvent(input$quiz_mark_incorrect, quiz_advance("incorrect"))

  output$quiz_counter <- renderText({ paste(rv$quiz_cur,"/",length(rv$quiz_deck)) })
  output$quiz_cat     <- renderText({ quiz_card()$category })

  output$quiz_progress <- renderUI({
    n <- length(rv$quiz_deck); done <- rv$quiz_cur - 1L
    pct <- if(n>0) round(100*done/n) else 0
    tagList(
      div(class="progress-outer", div(class="progress-inner",style=paste0("width:",pct,"%;"))),
      div(class="progress-txt", paste0(done," / ",n," answered (",pct,"%)"))
    )
  })

  output$quiz_panel <- renderUI({
    card  <- quiz_card()
    score <- rv$quiz_score

    # Colour and label for the score meter
    bar_col <- if(score >= 60) "#27ae60" else if(score >= 30) "#f39c12" else "#e74c3c"
    verdict <- if(score >= 60) paste0("Good match — ",score,"% keyword overlap")
               else if(score >= 30) paste0("Partial match — ",score,"% keyword overlap")
               else if(score > 0)   paste0("Low match — ",score,"% keyword overlap")
               else ""

    tagList(
      div(class="quiz-shell",
        div(class="quiz-counter", textOutput("quiz_counter", inline=TRUE)),
        div(class="quiz-badge",   textOutput("quiz_cat",     inline=TRUE)),

        # Term
        div(class="quiz-term", card$term),
        div(class="quiz-cat",  card$category),

        # Answer textarea (always rendered; hidden after submit via CSS pointer-events)
        tags$textarea(
          id          = "quiz_answer",
          class       = "quiz-input",
          placeholder = "Type your definition here, then press Submit Answer…",
          style       = if (rv$quiz_revealed) "opacity:0.55;pointer-events:none;" else ""
        ),

        # Score meter (only after submit)
        if (rv$quiz_revealed) {
          div(class="score-meter-wrap",
            div(class="score-meter-outer",
              div(class="score-meter-inner",
                  style=paste0("width:",score,"%;background:",bar_col,";"))),
            div(class="score-label", style=paste0("color:",bar_col,";"), verdict)
          )
        },

        # Model answer (only after submit)
        if (rv$quiz_revealed) {
          div(class="answer-box",
            div(class="answer-box-title", "Model Answer"),
            card$definition
          )
        }
      ),

      # Buttons
      if (!rv$quiz_revealed) {
        div(class="btn-row",
          tags$button("Submit Answer", class="btn-submit",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('quiz_submit_btn',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
        )
      } else {
        div(class="btn-row",
          tags$button("✓ Mark Correct", class="btn-correct",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('quiz_mark_correct',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});"),
          tags$button("✗ Mark Incorrect", class="btn-incorrect",
            onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('quiz_mark_incorrect',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
        )
      }
    )
  })

  output$completion_ui <- renderUI({
    n   <- length(rv$quiz_deck)
    cor <- sum(rv$statuses[rv$quiz_deck]=="correct")
    pct <- if(n>0) round(100*cor/n) else 0
    grade <- if(pct>=80)"🎉 Excellent!" else if(pct>=60)"👍 Good effort" else "📖 Keep reviewing"
    div(class="completion-panel",
      div(class="completion-title", grade),
      div(class="completion-pct",   paste0(pct,"%")),
      div(style="font-size:18px;color:#555;", paste0(cor," correct out of ",n," cards")),
      br(),
      tags$button("Retake Quiz", class="btn-restart",
        style="font-size:16px;padding:12px 32px;",
        onclick="Shiny.setInputValue('restart_btn',Math.random(),{priority:'event'});")
    )
  })

  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # RESULTS
  # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  results_df <- reactive({
    cat <- input$cat_filter; idx <- seq_len(N)
    if (!is.null(cat) && cat != "ALL")
      idx <- idx[sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$category == cat)]
    data.frame(
      Term     = sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$term),
      Category = sapply(idx, function(i) flashcards[[i]]$category),
      Status   = rv$statuses[idx],
      stringsAsFactors=FALSE
    )
  })

  output$r_total   <- renderText({ nrow(results_df()) })
  output$r_correct <- renderText({ sum(results_df()$Status=="correct") })
  output$r_wrong   <- renderText({ sum(results_df()$Status=="incorrect") })
  output$r_skipped <- renderText({ sum(results_df()$Status=="skipped") })
  output$r_pct <- renderText({
    df <- results_df(); seen <- df$Status %in% c("correct","incorrect")
    if (!any(seen)) return("–")
    paste0(round(100*sum(df$Status=="correct")/sum(seen)),"%")
  })

  output$results_tbl <- renderUI({
    df <- results_df()

    status_html <- function(s) {
      switch(s,
        "correct"   = '<span style="color:#27ae60;font-weight:700;">✓ Correct</span>',
        "incorrect" = '<span style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:700;">✗ Review</span>',
        "skipped"   = '<span style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:700;">→ Skipped</span>',
                      '<span style="color:#aaa;font-weight:600;">Unseen</span>'
      )
    }

    ord <- c("incorrect","unseen","skipped","correct")
    df  <- df[order(match(df$Status, ord)), ]

    rows <- paste0(
      apply(df, 1, function(r) {
        paste0(
          "<tr>",
          "<td style='padding:9px 12px;font-size:13px;border-bottom:1px solid #f0f0f0;'>",
          htmltools::htmlEscape(r["Term"]), "</td>",
          "<td style='padding:9px 12px;font-size:13px;border-bottom:1px solid #f0f0f0;'>",
          htmltools::htmlEscape(r["Category"]), "</td>",
          "<td style='padding:9px 12px;font-size:13px;border-bottom:1px solid #f0f0f0;'>",
          status_html(r["Status"]), "</td>",
          "</tr>"
        )
      }),
      collapse=""
    )

    HTML(paste0(
      '<table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;">',
      '<thead><tr>',
      '<th style="background:#f0f4fa;color:#1a3c5e;padding:10px 12px;font-size:13px;',
      'border-bottom:2px solid #dde6f0;text-align:left;">Term</th>',
      '<th style="background:#f0f4fa;color:#1a3c5e;padding:10px 12px;font-size:13px;',
      'border-bottom:2px solid #dde6f0;text-align:left;">Category</th>',
      '<th style="background:#f0f4fa;color:#1a3c5e;padding:10px 12px;font-size:13px;',
      'border-bottom:2px solid #dde6f0;text-align:left;">Status</th>',
      '</tr></thead>',
      '<tbody>', rows, '</tbody>',
      '</table>'
    ))
  })

}  # closes server function

shinyApp(
    options = list(height = "900px"),
    ui=ui, server=server)
```
